key_points: Brazeale Hall is freshman dorm; brotherhood atmosphere noted by alumni
No 24-hour study hall or computer lab available on campus
Buildings described as lacking traditional collegiate aesthetic
key_quotes: Morehouse College is missing a 24 hours study hall for their students / Computer Lab. Every college has one, but Morehouse College does not.
Looks clean but the buildings don't have that collegiate look like a lot of colleges and universities. Lots of parking lots.
so much brotherhood emanating from this video!

key_points: Greek life dominates campus culture; non-D9 students can feel excluded
AUC ecosystem means social life spans Spelman, Clark Atlanta, and Morehouse
Dress culture is serious — orientation 'fit checks' are real events
key_quotes: Can't tell a brother nothing when he pop on the scene hanging a suit the way he do.
I don't like how much HBCUs center fraternities and sororities — what about SGA, newspaper, debate team?
these type of videos from you motivate & give me sm inspiration about college and just living life in general

key_points: No specific dining complaints surfaced, but minimal data available
Campus expansion ongoing but food facilities not highlighted in tours
No notable praise for dining options in any source reviewed
key_quotes: no tv's- lose the flat screens- increase the books- stay focus.
It's amazing how much Da House has expanded from 10 acres and 800 students since when I was a student from 1962 to 66.

key_points: Class and legacy hierarchy creates real stress for first-gen, low-income students
Homophobia reported as a campus issue; one HBCU student died from targeted harassment
No mentions of counseling resources or mental health support in any source
key_quotes: I hate being a student at a 'prestigious' HBCU who's not a rich kid. Everybody is Jack and Jill, entire family D9, 4th generation student — makes me not want to be here.
PSA: Humility goes a very long way. Be proud of your achievements, yes but always remember that what you do is not who you are.
NCCU student dies from targeted anonymous harassment, recounts feeling excluded due to homophobia and not being D9.

key_points: No in-state tuition — private school costs hit every student equally hard
Robert F. Smith's 2019 loan payoff was extraordinary but a one-time event
Arthur Blank's $50M gap scholarship fund targets students whose aid runs short
key_quotes: Morehouse is not an inexpensive place to go to school, and there is no 'in-state' tuition.
Howard offered me 20k out of their 58k cost of attendance. Spelman offered me $0. Tuskegee offered me a full ride. Apply everywhere!
Spelman gives little to no aid to middle class families, I assume based on low endowment funds purposed for it.

key_points: Morehouse is one of only 7 HBCUs with a graduation rate above 50%
Strong legacy in theology, humanities, and pre-professional tracks; physics noted
ACT average of 21-23 puts it in mid-tier academically among national colleges
key_quotes: Spelman was the highest at 75% and Howard coming in second — Morehouse makes that short list of 7 HBCUs above 50%.
I'm a high-school senior from GA who just got all of his decisions back — Morehouse College (physics) vs UC Davis (physics).
These are young college students. Not thugs nor dope dealers like the media would have you believe.

key_points: Biden's 2024 commencement speech got mixed reviews; students visibly unenthused
Honorary degree given despite federal funding cuts — drew student criticism
No major admin scandals surfaced specific to Morehouse in this data set
key_quotes: He cut their funding, basically tell them they are not going to amount to ish and they gave him an honorary degree... Sounds about right.
I hope other mega-wealthy people step up and do likewise.
A professor at Morehouse noted: 'Removing a mountain of student loan debt for hundreds of graduates is some of the best use of money I can imagine.'

key_points: Atlanta University Center location gives access to Spelman, Clark Atlanta, shared resources
Campus criticized for lacking traditional collegiate architecture; lots of parking lots
Historic buildings like Graves Hall and King Chapel carry deep cultural weight
key_quotes: Looks clean but the buildings don't have that collegiate look like a lot of colleges and universities. Maybe Graves Hall, but that's about it. Lots of parking lots.
I can still remember the day I landed on campus for orientation. When I close my eyes, I can still smell the smell of King Chapel.
I love that the AUC holds so much history and tradition!

key_points: Robert F. Smith's loan payoff signals high-powered alumni network engagement
Morehouse men enter professional world with strong brand recognition nationally
No specific internship placement data or career center complaints surfaced
key_quotes: You have given the Class of 2019 a head start in any profession and life without that crippling student loan debt.
This person just changed the lives of 400 young men in ways they won't realize for years. He may have CHANGED THEIR FAMILY TREE.
Thank you for giving these 400 men the opportunity to build generational wealth.

key_points: High tuition with no in-state option makes cost a serious barrier
Prestigious brand and alumni network may justify cost for some students
Federal HBCU funding cuts under current administration add financial uncertainty
key_quotes: Morehouse is not an inexpensive place to go to school, and there is no 'in-state' tuition.
Department Of Education Cancels $350M In HBCU Grants — institutions on 'financial code red.'
Black elitism has too many of the parents in a chokehold — Tuskegee and Hampton offered full rides while Howard gave $20k of $58k.

key_points: Brotherhood and legacy culture are genuinely powerful and emotionally resonant

Honor to be able to attend this graduation, and also honored to say that I will be attending THE MOREHOUSE COLLEGE this fall.

King Chapel and historic traditions create rare spiritual and cultural depth
Alumni donor culture is exceptional — billionaires actively give back to graduates
